author: Rick Middleton
msg:
I mean that is the idea.. the ULOs do the work and we UNOs take a % of the earnings.  The system is not designed that we run 200 phones ourselves and do 200 lots of manual tasks ourselves..

For people in parts of Africa earning maybe $5 per day, or place like Indonesia or Philippines where they may be on $15 per day the earnings for these sort of manual tasks will likely be pretty respectable for the amount of time they need to put in...   unlike in Europe or USA where it is a much smaller fraction of a daily wage

author: LIIICHT
msg:
I guess you meant ULOs.
The problem with S&R for disadvantaged countries is the additional cost involved running it. Some of the sims are over $20 a month to buy and participate.

author: Rick Middleton
msg: Yes the upfront cost can be steep - but at least for Scouts wasn't the plan that would be refunded?
